title: Bassovia velutina
taxonomy: Bassovia velutina is a species of plant in the family Solanaceae. This family includes a wide range of plants, commonly known as the nightshade family. Members of the Solanaceae family include plants such as potatoes, tomatoes, and eggplants. Bassovia velutina is further classified into the genus Bassovia, which is a group of plants native to South America. The classification of Bassovia velutina is an important aspect of understanding its characteristics and evolution.

Its bark and leaves are used to treat fever, rheumatism, and other inflammatory conditions.
